title: Monetary policy
description: An exercise providing students an opportunity to apply their understanding of monetary policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
Monetary policy is a critical economic tool used by the Federal Reserve (the Fed) to manage the money supply and interest rates. Its primary goals are full employment, stable prices, and economic growth.

**If the Fed lowers the interest rate, which goal(s) is it trying to achieve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: full employment
stable prices
economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
Monetary policy is a critical economic tool used by the Federal Reserve to manage the money supply and interest rates. Its primary goals are full employment, stable prices, and economic growth.

**If the Fed increases the interest rate, which goal(s) is it trying to achieve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: full employment
stable prices
economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
Monetary policy is a critical economic tool used by the Federal Reserve to manage the money supply and interest rates. Its primary goals are full employment, stable prices, and economic growth.

**If the Fed buys government securities on the open market, which goal(s) is it trying to achieve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: full employment
stable prices
economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
Monetary policy is a critical economic tool used by the Federal Reserve to manage the money supply and interest rates. Its primary goals are full employment, stable prices, and economic growth.

**If the Fed sells government securities on the open market, which goal(s) is it trying to achieve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: full employment
stable prices
economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the tools of monetary policy is the required reserve ratio.

**What is the purpose of the required reserve ratio?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It controls the amount of money banks must keep on hand to ensure they have enough funds to meet customer withdrawals.
It determines the interest rate that banks charge each other for short-term loans.
It involves the Federal Reserve buying and selling government securities to influence the money supply.
It allows the Federal Reserve to collect interest on excess reserves.

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the tools of monetary policy is the federal funds rate.

**What is a major function of the federal funds rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It determines the percentage of deposits banks must keep on hand.
It sets the rate at which banks charge each other for short-term loans, influencing overall interest rates.
It allows for buying and selling of government securities to increase or decrease the money supply.
It allows the Federal Reserve to collect interest on excess reserves.

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the tools of monetary policy is open market operations.

**What is open market operations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the percentage of deposits banks must keep on hand.
It is the interest rate banks charge each other for short-term loans.
It is buying and selling government securities to influence the amount of money circulating in the economy.
It is the rate the Federal Reserve pays banks for holding excess reserves.

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the tools of monetary policy is interest on reserves.

**What is interest on reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the amount of money banks must keep on hand to ensure they can meet customer withdrawals.
It is the rate at which banks charge each other for short-term loans.
It is buying and selling government securities to influence the money supply.
It pays banks interest on their excess reserves, encouraging them to hold more or less money in reserve.

assessment_items.question_content:
Laila reads that the Federal Reserve has reduced the required reserve ratio.

**What is the likely economic impact of this action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation will decrease as less money circulates.
Unemployment will decrease as more money becomes available for lending.
Consumer spending will fall as interest rates increase.
Economic growth will slow down as borrowing becomes more expensive.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carlos learns that the Federal Reserve is selling government securities on the open market.

**What is a potential economic impact of this action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Unemployment will likely increase as the money supply contracts.
Inflation will rise, leading to higher consumer prices.
Economic growth will increase as more money becomes available for businesses.
Interest rates will fall, encouraging more consumer spending.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fatima hears that the Federal Reserve has lowered the federal funds rate.

**What is a potential consequence of this action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation could increase as borrowing becomes easier.
Unemployment will increase as businesses cut back on hiring.
Consumer spending will fall as interest rates rise.
The economy will contract as borrowing becomes more expensive.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jamal learns that the Federal Reserve has raised the interest rate on reserves.

**What is a potential economic impact of this action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Unemployment could rise due to decreased business investment.
Inflation will increase as more money circulates in the economy.
Economic growth will rise, leading to more job creation.
Consumer spending will increase as interest rates drop.
